eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
eleven: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 11, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
elevon: noun. A movable surface at the trailing edge of a tailless airplane (such as the space shuttle) that provides pitch and roll control.
elope: verb. Of a married or engaged person, to run away from home with a paramour.
enol: noun. An organic compound containing a hydroxyl group bonded to a carbon atom which is doubly bonded to another carbon atom.
envelop: verb. To surround, enclose or enfold.
envelope: noun. A paper or cardboard wrapper used to enclose small, flat items, especially letters, for mailing.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
epee: noun. A sharp-pointed dueling sword with a bell-shaped guard, used (with the end blunted) in sport fencing.
lee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lyélé.
leone: noun. A unit of currency of Sierra Leone, divided into 100 cents.
lev: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lamma.
levee: noun. An elevated ridge of deposited sediment on the banks of a river, formed by the river's overflow at times of high discharge.
lop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lopa.
lope: verb. To travel an easy pace with long strides.
love: noun. A deep caring for the existence of another.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
novel: adjective. Newly made, formed or evolved; having no precedent; of recent origin; new.
ole: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Olekha.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ope: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Old Persian.
oven: noun. A chamber used for baking or heating.
pee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Taje.
peel: verb. To remove the skin or outer covering of.
peen: noun. The (often spherical) end of the head of a hammer opposite the main hammering end.
pen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Penesak.
peon: noun. A lowly person; a peasant or serf; a labourer who is obliged to do menial work.
pone: noun. A writ in law used by the superior courts to remove cases from inferior courts.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veep: noun. Abbreviation of Vice President of the United States; the office of Vice President of the United States, especially during an election
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
vole: noun. Any of a large number of species of small rodents of the tribes Arvicolini, Ellobiusini, Clethrionomyini, Pliomyini, Phenacomyini and
